top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra Agent ID Flaw Enabled Tenant Takeover via Privilege Escalation
top_stories.summary: A vulnerability in Microsoft Entra Agent ID allowed attackers to escalate privileges and take over tenants through Service Principal abuse. Microsoft has fully patched the flaw.

top_stories.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
top_stories.summary: Microsoft will begin rolling out passkey support for Microsoft Entra-protected resources on Windows devices starting in late April. The feature enables phishing-resistant passwordless authentication for Entra-managed environments.

top_stories.title: 'Identity is the new battleground': Why your IT helpdesk is suddenly getting a lot of bizarre calls
top_stories.summary: Okta-targeted vishing attacks are exploiting IT helpdesk staff through impersonation and urgency tactics to gain unauthorized access. These social engineering attacks manipulate support personnel into bypassing authentication controls, enabling attackers to escalate privileges and cause large-scale data breaches.

top_stories.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Help Desk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
top_stories.summary: A threat actor cluster called UNC6692 is using Microsoft Teams to impersonate IT help desk personnel and deploy the SNOW malware suite through social engineering. The attack relies on victims accepting Teams chat invitations from attacker-controlled accounts posing as internal IT support staff.

top_stories.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
top_stories.summary: The Bitwarden CLI package (@bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0) was compromised as part of an ongoing supply chain attack campaign discovered by JFrog and Socket. Malicious code was injected into a file called 'bw1.js' within the package, targeting users of the command-line interface for the Bitwarden password manager.

news.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
news.summary: The article addresses the governance and authorization challenges posed by AI agents in enterprise security, framing the core issue as one of delegated authority and identity management for non-human actors. It focuses on how AI agents receive, exercise, and should be governed under IAM principles such as continuous observability and authorization controls.

news.title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
news.summary: The article examines how DORA's Article 9 establishes authentication and access control as legal obligations for EU financial institutions. It covers what the regulation specifically requires around credential management and the consequences of non-compliance or control failures.

news.title: The New World ID: Proof of Human for the AI Era Scales Across the Digital Platforms People and Businesses Use Every Day
news.summary: World announced the next generation of World ID, a proof-of-human protocol, with Okta among the digital platform partners bringing the technology to businesses, AI agents, and consumers. The integration with Okta and other platforms positions World ID as an identity verification layer aimed at distinguishing humans from AI agents in authentication workflows.

news.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
news.summary: Vercel identified additional compromised customer accounts stemming from a breach linked to Context.ai that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems. The expanded investigation reviewed additional compromise indicators and analyzed requests to the Vercel network and environment to uncover the affected accounts.

news.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
news.summary: Specops Software has published research showing how regular password resets can be exploited by attackers through helpdesk social engineering to achieve full account compromise. The analysis details how seemingly legitimate reset requests can bypass security controls, challenging the assumption that frequent password resets improve security.

news.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
news.summary: Researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network for AI agents, exposed 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents via an open database. The breach also revealed plaintext third-party credentials including OpenAI API keys within private messages, illustrating cross-application permission stacking risks in agentic AI environments.

news.title: Consumer Identity and Access Management Global Market Forecast Report 2026-2036, Profiles of Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, Amazon Web Services, Accenture
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global Consumer Identity and Access Management (CIAM) market will grow from USD 12.6 billion in 2026 to USD 40.2 billion by 2036, representing a CAGR of 12.2%. The report profiles major CIAM vendors including Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, AWS, and Accenture.

news.title: Non-Human Identity Access Management Market Global Forecast Report 2026-2036: Opportunities in Adoption of Zero Trust Architecture, Cloud-native Applications, and Regulatory Compliance Requirements
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global non-human identity (NHI) access management market will grow from $12.2 billion in 2026 to $38.8 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers market dynamics across regions and sectors, with growth drivers including zero trust architecture adoption, cloud-native applications, and regulatory compliance requirements.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. His admitted crimes involved SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in cryptocurrency theft from investors.

news.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
news.summary: A 2025 threat report highlights increasing attacker focus on exploiting weaknesses in MFA workflows and using compromised credentials to conduct phishing from trusted accounts. The attacks specifically target authentication trust mechanisms, combining credential theft with MFA bypass techniques to undermine identity security controls.

news.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
news.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool led to a data breach involving stolen OAuth tokens. Security researchers are highlighting OAuth tokens as an emerging critical attack surface for lateral movement in enterprise environments.

news.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
news.summary: Threat actors previously using the Tycoon 2FA phishing kit have shifted to device code phishing as an attack method. The technique exploits legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to bypass MFA and capture account access tokens without requiring victims to enter credentials on a fake page.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to targeted systems.
